back_translation: The second lot came out for Simeon, for the tribe of the sons of Simeon according to their clans; and their inheritance was in the midst of the inheritance of the sons of Judah.
And in their inheritance was: Beersheba and Sheba and Moladah,
and Hazar-shual and Balah and Ezem,
and Eltolad and Bethul and Hormah,
and Ziklag and Beth-marcaboth and Hazar-susah,
and Beth-lebaoth and Sharuhen — thirteen cities and their villages.
Ain, Rimmon and Ether and Ashan — four cities and their villages,
and all the villages that were around these cities as far as Baalath-beer (Ramah of the Negev). This was the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Simeon according to their clans.

From the portion of the sons of Judah was the inheritance of the sons of Simeon, because the share of the sons of Judah was too large for them; so the sons of Simeon received an inheritance in the midst of their inheritance.
The third lot came up for the sons of Zebulun according to their clans; and the border of their inheritance was as far as Sarid.
Their border went up westward, even Maralah, and met Dabbesheth, and met the wadi which is opposite Jokneam.
It turned from Sarid eastward toward the sunrise to the border of Chisloth-tabor, and went out to Daberath, and went up to Japhia.
And from there it crossed eastward to Gath-hepher, Eth-kazin, and went out to Rimmon, which curves to Neah.
And the border circled it from the north to Hannathon; and its outgoings were the Valley of Iphtah-el,
and Kattath and Nahalal and Shimron and Idalah and Bethlehem — twelve cities and their villages.
This was the inheritance of the sons of Zebulun according to their clans — these cities and their villages.
For Issachar the fourth lot came out, for the sons of Issachar according to their clans.
And their border was: Jezreel and Chesulloth and Shunem,
and Hapharaim and Shion and Anaharath,
and Rabbith and Kishion and Ebez,
and Remeth and En-gannim and En-haddah and Beth-pazzez.
The border touched Tabor and Shahazumah and Beth-shemesh, and the outgoings of their border were the Jordan — sixteen cities and their villages.
This was the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Issachar according to their clans — the cities and their villages.
The fifth lot came out for the tribe of the sons of Asher according to their clans.
And their border was: Helkath and Hali and Beten and Achshaph,
and Allammelech and Amad and Mishal. It met at Carmel westward and Shihor-libnath.
Then it turned eastward toward Beth-dagon and met Zebulun and the Valley of Iphtah-el to the north, Beth-emek and Neiel; and it went out to Cabul on the left,
and Ebron and Rehob and Hammon and Kanah, as far as Greater Sidon.
The border returned to Ramah and as far as the fortified city of Tyre; the border returned to Hosah, and its outgoings were at the sea from the region of Achzib,
and Ummah and Aphek and Rehob — twenty-two cities and their villages.
This was the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Asher according to their clans — these cities and their villages.
For the sons of Naphtali the sixth lot came out, for the sons of Naphtali according to their clans.
And their border was: from Heleph, from the great tree at Zaanannim, and Adami-nekeb and Jabneel, as far as Lakkum; and its outgoings were the Jordan.
The border returned westward to Aznoth-tabor and went out from there to Hukkok; it met Zebulun from the south, and Asher it met from the west, and Judah at the Jordan toward the sunrise (east).
The fortified cities were Ziddim, Zer, and Hammath, Rakkath, and Chinnereth,
and Adamah and Ramah and Hazor,
and Kedesh and Edrei and En-hazor,
and Iron and Migdal-el, Horem and Beth-anath and Beth-shemesh — nineteen cities and their villages.
This was the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali according to their clans — the cities and their villages.
For the tribe of the sons of Dan according to their clans the seventh lot came out.
And the border of their inheritance was: Zorah and Eshtaol and Ir-shemesh,
and Shaalabbin and Aijalon and Ithlah,
and Elon and Timnah and Ekron,
and Eltekeh and Gibbethon and Baalath,
and Jehud and Bene-berak and Gath-rimmon,
and Me-jarkon and Rakkon, with the border opposite Joppa.

And the border of the sons of Dan went out from them; the sons of Dan went up and fought with Leshem and captured it, and struck it with the edge of the sword and possessed it and dwelt in it; and they called Leshem 'Dan,' after the name of Dan their father.
This was the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Dan according to their clans — these cities and their villages.
When they had finished apportioning the land into its borders, the sons of Israel gave an inheritance to Joshua son of Nun in their midst.
According to the mouth (command) of the LORD they gave him the city which he asked — Timnath-serah in the hill country of Ephraim; and he built the city and dwelt in it.

These are the inheritances which Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the sons of Israel apportioned by lot at Shiloh before the LORD, at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. So they finished dividing the land.
